mechanism_narrative: An increase in real retail sales signals robust consumer demand, which translates into higher expected corporate revenues and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns do not significantly outpace negative-return months during RSAFS increases (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate below 2.5% signals that monetary policy is accommodative in response to severe financial stress, and the associated recession risk generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of months when FEDFUNDS is below 2.5%, or the mean triggered return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in UNRATE and decline in PAYEMS indicates accelerating labor market deterioration that amplifies negative income effects beyond either signal in isolation, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between UNRATE changes and PAYEMS changes is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) in explaining 20-day S&P 500 returns, or the interaction-driven portfolio Rank IC falls below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in industrial production after two consecutive monthly declines signals a normalization in manufacturing output during a post-shock recovery, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO positive month-over-month change following two consecutive declines
failure_condition: Positive 5-day S&P 500 returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered post-shock recovery episodes, defined as periods following a 15% or greater S&P 500 drawdown.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below 1000 thousand units signal a severe contraction in residential investment and construction employment that transmits into broad credit losses and negative equity risk premia, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 60-day S&P 500 returns occur in fewer than 55% of months when HOUST is below 1000, or the mean return during triggered periods is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in consumer prices that exceeds the prior three-month average signals building inflationary pressure that erodes corporate profit margins through higher input costs, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or months with accelerating CPIAUCSL do not produce significantly lower 20-day returns than decelerating months (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate of more than 0.2 percentage points over two months signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate equity de-risking and earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.2 percentage points over 2 months
failure_condition: Triggered episodes produce positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 45% of instances, or the mean return during triggered periods is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP reflects the aggregate demand environment; positive GDPC1 growth indicates expanding economic output that supports corporate revenue and earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive GDPC1 growth quartiles fail to monotonically predict higher 60-day S&P 500 returns.
